raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to review the section includes assessing whether the section-level claim about robustness and selective fusion is framed appropriately.","R1.2":"A review should check whether the causal and argumentative chain from benchmark results to robustness conclusions is coherent.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance and ablation claims, so verifying whether those claims are supported by the reported results is load-bearing.","R1.6":"A general section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, concision, and register.","RX.1":"The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only the section text and not tables, experimental details, or external sources.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about leadership and robustness, so avoiding unsupported overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The task is only to review this section, so responsibility must remain bounded to the provided artifact and requested review scope.","RX.4":"The review should avoid downstream harm by flagging claims that could mislead readers about robustness or state-of-the-art status.","RX.5":"Any projected review responsibility should preserve traceability to the section's stated claims, numbers, baselines, and ablation statements."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to review the section includes assessing whether the section-level claim about robustness and selective fusion is framed appropriately.
rationale.R1.2: A review should check whether the causal and argumentative chain from benchmark results to robustness conclusions is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and ablation claims, so verifying whether those claims are supported by the reported results is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, concision, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only the section text and not tables, experimental details, or external sources.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about leadership and robustness, so avoiding unsupported overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to review this section, so responsibility must remain bounded to the provided artifact and requested review scope.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid downstream harm by flagging claims that could mislead readers about robustness or state-of-the-art status.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should preserve traceability to the section's stated claims, numbers, baselines, and ablation statements.
